title: The Clock Tower Becomes the Key
description: On pages 13–16, this part focuses on the clock tower becomes the key, keeping the scene centered on the conflict, choices, and stakes of the Alabasta arc.
scene: The Clock Tower Becomes the Key. Luffy comes back for another fight with Crocodile, now armed with the will to keep standing. This section presents the immediate scene beat before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: Put up the white flags of Surren- Der this instant, Royalist Soldiers!

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: What will come of this country if we do that!?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Listen to me! We wish neither victory nor to fight!

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: All we want is to stop this war!

dialogue.speaker: Pell
dialogue.text: That this whole fight is pointless!

dialogue.speaker: Pell
dialogue.text: There is absolutely no reason to fight anymore!

dialogue.speaker: Pell
dialogue.text: We cannot spill anymore needless blood!

dialogue.speaker: Chaka
dialogue.text: The King is Right Before Us!

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: I can see the clock tower!

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: We're almost at the Palace square!

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: The Royalists are waving white flags!

summary: This part develops the clock tower becomes the key and pushes the Alabasta conflict closer to its next decisive turn.
